qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Integer overflow in WindowUpdate SYN credit calculation allows remote denial of service
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding describes integer overflow in WindowUpdate SYN credit calculation at connection.rs:730 (`credit + DEFAULT_CREDIT` u32 overflow). The advisory describes a different vulnerability: a Data frame with SYN and oversized body (len > DEFAULT_CREDIT) that triggers `remove().expect("stream not found")` panic. Different frame type (WindowUpdate vs Data), different code path (credit arithmetic vs body-size validation and stream cleanup), and different root cause (integer overflow vs reachable assertion on stream-not-found).

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding matches the advisory-described vulnerability. The advisory describes a panic in the Rust yamux crate triggered by a malformed Data frame with SYN flag and oversized body (len > DEFAULT_CREDIT), where stream state is created before validation and cleanup calls remove().expect() on a missing stream. Finding window_update_syn_credit_overflow describes a different vulnerability in the same crate: integer overflow in WindowUpdate SYN credit calculation (different frame type, different code path, different root cause).
evidence: Advisory root cause: Data|SYN frame with len > DEFAULT_CREDIT triggers remove().expect("stream not found") panic during stream cleanup after oversized-body validation failure.
window_update_syn_credit_overflow root cause: WindowUpdate|SYN frame with credit near u32::MAX triggers integer overflow in credit + DEFAULT_CREDIT arithmetic at connection.rs:730.
Different frame types: advisory uses Data (tag=0), finding uses WindowUpdate (tag=1).
Different panic triggers: advisory uses reachable assertion (CWE-617), finding uses arithmetic overflow (CWE-190).
